message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:p>A new Universal rule for Covid 19 data is derived in this paper using the SIR model.</jats:p>
                <jats:p>It relates infection and removal rates and is validated by the global Covid 19 data. Over 186,000 data points, from 190 countries and the states of the US, for the period April 1 to December 12, 2020 - fall on a single line, as the Universal rule predicts, transcending geography, ethnicity and race.</jats:p>
                <jats:p>The Universal rule requires that Herd immunity begin when just 25% of the population is vaccinated. With the anticipated 100 million vaccinations in the first 100 days of the Biden administration, Herd immunity may be imminent in the US.</jats:p>
                <jats:p>The Universal rule promotes a temporary stasis with continuing infections and hospitalizations and becomes a barrier to runaway infections, making it practically impossible to reach Herd immunity, as Sweden discovered. Reduced infected population seems to be a third option to stifle the epidemic - a little known accomplishment, first by North Dakota and subsequently by twelve other U.S. states, including South Dakota.</jats:p>
title: Universal Rule for Covid 19 and Herd Immunity in the US

reference.article-title: Chaos and the dynamics of biological populations

reference.article-title: Exact analytical solutions of the Susceptible-Infected-Recovered (SIR) epidemic model and of the SIR model with equal death and birth rates
